source.name: Level 2: 8.1 Knowledge Check - Sending messages from an action
source.instructions: **How does an action send a text message to the user?**

answers.answer: By returning a string from run()
By writing to the domain responses section
By calling dispatcher.utter_message(text=message) inside run(); the dispatcher is passed into run() as a parameter
By adding a step to the flow that references utter_*

source.learningObjectives: SWBAT identify how to send messages from an action
source.guidance: Inside your action's run() method, you receive a CollectingDispatcher (usually named dispatcher). To send a message to the user, you call dispatcher.utter_message(text="Your message here"). The run() method should also return [] (an empty list) at the end. You do not return the message string; you use the dispatcher to send it.
